rationale: Both evaluations reach essentially the same conclusion — the candidate lacks C++ on Windows, SQL Server/Oracle, and fixed-income/capital-markets domain experience — and list identical strengths. The variant simply elaborates more (citing the 2014–2015 National Instruments C++ stint and DLang) and reframes one concern around tech-stack alignment. The 1-point score difference and the reworded reasoning are within ordinary run-to-run variation and do not reference or key off the changed employer names (Stripe, Shopify, Datadog); none of the variant's reasoning credits or penalizes the candidate based on company prestige.
